meta.title: Plaza de Francia | French Plaza - Panama City
meta.description: Explore Plaza de Francia in Casco Antiguo, Panama City. A historical tourist attraction with rich cultural heritage and magnificent views.

hero.title: Plaza de Francia
hero.subtitle: French Plaza · Casco Antiguo, Panama City

intro.title: Explore Plaza de Francia
intro.description:
Located at the southernmost tip of Casco Antiguo in Panama City, Plaza de Francia is a landmark that perfectly blends heavy history with extreme beauty. The obelisk at the center of the plaza is topped with a Gallic rooster, surrounded by marble busts and monument inscriptions, solemnly commemorating the tens of thousands of French engineers and laborers who sacrificed their lives to build the Panama Canal at the end of the 19th century, as well as the scientists who made outstanding contributions to conquering yellow fever.

Today, this is the most popular leisure destination in the old town. The outer edge of the plaza is attached to the ancient Spanish seawall fortifications (Las Bóvedas). Strolling along the tree-lined waterfront promenade, you can not only admire the exquisite handicrafts sold by local indigenous people, but also overlook the Amador Causeway and the incredibly stunning modern skyscraper skyline of Panama City across the azure Bay of Panama. Here, centuries of historical vicissitudes and the prosperity of a modern metropolis are separated by just a strip of water.

visitGuide.title: Visiting Tips
items: The plaza is open 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
Comfortable walking shoes are recommended for exploring the old town
Bring a camera to capture the beautiful scenery of the plaza and surrounding colonial architecture
You can combine your visit with other historical attractions in Casco Antiguo

alsoKnownAs.title: Highlights
items: Waterfront Promenade and Domes (Las Bóvedas): Plaza de Francia is not just a flat plaza. Its outer edge is an ancient Spanish defensive seawall, with the famous 'domes' (former prisons and warehouses) below. Visitors can stroll along the seawall promenade, which is one of the biggest selling points of the attraction.

Excellent City Skyline View: Standing on the seawall of Plaza de Francia, you can enjoy a dead-angle-free view of Panama City's modern skyscraper skyline, the Amador Causeway, and the Bridge of the Americas across the Bay of Panama.

Monument Details: The center of the plaza is not just a 'monument'. The top is crowned with a Gallic Rooster representing France, surrounded by sculptures commemorating the workers and doctors who sacrificed their lives to yellow fever and malaria (such as Cuban doctor Carlos Finlay).
Prime Location: Located in the heart of Casco Antiguo, surrounded by exquisite colonial architecture.

route.title: Tour Route
route.overview: The ideal route to explore Plaza de Francia:
steps: Enter from the main entrance and enjoy the central open space and green trees.
Stroll around the park and feel the daily life and cultural atmosphere of local residents.
Visit nearby historical buildings like Catedral Metropolitana / Iglesia San José.
Rest at nearby cafes or restaurants and taste local cuisine.
supplements: It's recommended to go during the day when the light is best, suitable for taking photos and enjoying the park scenery.
You can combine your visit with Panama City's historical attractions, such as the Cathedral, museums, etc.
Please respect local culture and keep the park environment clean.

knowledge.title: Discover the Charm of Plaza de Francia

sections.title: Historical Significance
sections.content: Plaza de Francia is an important urban space and social landmark in Casco Antiguo, and has long been a favorite leisure place for local residents and tourists.

sections.title: Cultural Landscape
sections.content: It features beautiful green environments and rich cultural activities, making it an excellent place to experience Panama City's urban culture and lifestyle.

sections.title: Visiting Experience
sections.content: Strolling through Plaza de Francia, feeling Panama's relaxed lifestyle and beautiful urban scenery, is an unforgettable cultural experience.

basicInfo.title: Basic Information
basicInfo.addressValue: C. 2a Oeste 85-81, Panamá, Provincia de Panamá, Panama

hours.title: Opening Hours
hours.outdoorTime: Open 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
hours.summerTime: Dec-Apr: Pleasant weather, ideal season for outdoor activities
hours.winterTime: May-Nov: Afternoon showers are common, recommended to visit in the morning
hours.warningTime: Recommended to verify latest information before visiting
hours.tip: Daytime visits offer the best experience

tickets.title: Useful Information
tickets.adultsPrice: Urban parking lots available, fees vary by location
tickets.cardPrice: The park is free and open to the public. It is recommended to combine your visit with the Metropolitan Cathedral of Panama City (Catedral Metropolitana) or San José Church (Iglesia San José - famous Golden Altar) in the old town.
tickets.officialNotice: For the latest visiting information, please refer to the
tickets.officialNoticeLinkLabel: Visit Panama (Panama Official Tourism Authority) Website

transport.title: Transportation
transport.airportDesc: About 30 minutes drive from Tocumen International Airport (PTY). Renting a car, taking a taxi, or taking the metro to the old town is recommended.
transport.selfDriveDesc: Located in Casco Antiguo, Panama City. Simply input 'Plaza de Francia' in navigation. Urban parking lots are available nearby.
transport.busDesc: The streets of Casco Antiguo in Panama City are narrow and parking is extremely difficult. It is highly recommended that visitors use ride-hailing apps to reach the edge of Casco Viejo and then walk in, which is the most authentic and safe advice. You can also take the Panama City metro to the surrounding area.
transport.intercityDesc: The plaza is located in the heart of Casco Antiguo, walkable from nearby attractions.
transport.cyclingDesc: Casco Antiguo has convenient transportation. Taxis, ride-sharing services, or sightseeing buses are available.
transport.tipsDesc: Plaza de Francia is an excellent place to experience Panama's history and culture in Casco Antiguo. Visiting during daylight hours is recommended.

items.name: Juan Carlos
items.text: The heart of Casco Antiguo. This park is beautiful and is an ideal place to relax and enjoy the local atmosphere.

items.name: María José
items.text: Beautiful park with a pleasant environment. Close to the Cathedral, it's a must-visit place when touring Casco Antiguo.

items.name: Roberto M.
items.text: Very good urban park. Suitable for walking and relaxing, with many shops and restaurants nearby, very convenient.

items.name: Ana Lucía
items.text: An iconic place in Casco Antiguo. The park is well-maintained and is an important part of local culture.

items.name: Carlos A.
items.text: Great place for leisure. You can rest here, watch people come and go, and feel the pace of life in Casco Antiguo.

items.name: Sofía M.
items.text: Beautiful park, full of Panamanian charm. An important stop on your Casco Antiguo trip, definitely worth experiencing.

items.name: Miguel R.
items.text: Interesting urban park. You can experience the local culture up close. Daytime visits recommended when the atmosphere is best.

items.name: Laura V.
items.text: An urban gem of Casco Antiguo. Central Park is an important place to understand Panama City's culture, definitely worth experiencing.

officialManagement.title: About Plaza de Francia
officialManagement.text: Plaza de Francia is a famous natural attraction in the Casco Antiguo, boasting spectacular mountain views and rich natural and cultural value. It is directly managed and maintained by the Panama Ministry of Culture (MiCultura).

historyTimeline.title: Historical Timeline

items.title: 1673: Spanish Fortifications
items.description: The Spaniards built fortifications here (the predecessor of Las Bóvedas), which was an important defense line for the old town against pirates and foreign invasions.

items.title: 1880s: French Canal Project
items.description: The French launched the Panama Canal project. Although it was not ultimately completed, they left valuable experience and lessons for later canal engineering.

items.title: 1920s: Plaza Inauguration
items.description: The plaza was officially inaugurated to commemorate the nearly 20,000 French and Caribbean laborers who sacrificed their lives to tropical diseases, as well as the engineers and doctors who contributed to the construction of the canal.

historyTimeline.guideContent: When visiting, you can start by entering from the main entrance and admiring the leisure spaces shaded by green trees; then visit nearby historical buildings like Catedral Metropolitana / Iglesia San José; if you have enough time, you can walk to nearby historical museums and cultural attractions. Finally, enjoy food in nearby cafes or restaurants and feel the relaxed pace of life in Casco Antiguo.
